shower.thought: The worst part about being betrayed isn't what they did—it's realizing you were wrong about who they were, which means you were also wrong about your own judgment.
You can't actually remember most of your life. You just remember the last time you remembered it, which is a copy of a copy of a copy.
If a tree falls in the forest and no one's around, it still makes a sound. But if your life is good and you never tell anyone about it, did it actually happen?
We spend decades learning how the world works, and by the time we're an expert, we're too tired to do anything about it.
Nostalgia is just your brain editing out all the times you were bored and uncomfortable.
You probably have a specific facial expression you make when you're concentrating, and you'll never see it naturally because it only exists when you're not looking in a mirror.
The scariest part of getting older isn't dying—it's realizing there are people who have never known a world without you being in it.
If you can imagine it, someone else has probably thought of it. The question is whether they acted on it before you.
Your pet has a completely different relationship with time than you do. To them, you're either always here or always gone.
We assume our senses show us reality, but they're actually just showing us what evolution decided we needed to survive.
The moment you become aware of your own mortality is the moment you're no longer fully living, because part of you is always a little bit dead.
You can only be truly surprised once per person, because after that you know what they're capable of.
Most of the time you think you're being original, you're actually just combining things other people created in a slightly different order.
If you're waiting for the right moment to do something, you're probably waiting for a moment that doesn't exist.
Your childhood bedroom exists in your memory in perfect detail, but if you walked into the real one tomorrow, you'd realize you've been remembering a completely different room.
The people who hurt you the most are usually the ones who understand you well enough to know exactly where to aim.
You can't want something you can't imagine, which means the best things in your life were things you didn't think to want yet.
Sleep is the only time your brain gets a break from proving you exist to yourself.
We call it "finding ourselves" but what we're really doing is becoming whoever we pretend to be convincingly enough.
If aliens are looking for intelligent life, they might have passed us by because they were looking for something too advanced to hide as well as we do.

motivational.thought: The person who says it can't be done should get out of the way of the person doing it. — Unknown
Your worst days make the best stories, but only if you survive them. — Unknown
Most people fail not because they aim too high and miss, but because they aim too low and hit. — Unknown
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most. — Unknown
You can't control the wind, but you can adjust your sails. — Unknown
The gap between who you are and who you want to be is filled with the things you're willing to do. — Unknown
Every expert was once a beginner who refused to quit. — Unknown
Progress is not about perfection. It's about showing up one day better than you showed up yesterday. — Unknown
Your limitations are often just beliefs you haven't questioned yet. — Unknown
The comfortable life you have now was built by the uncomfortable decisions you made before. — Unknown
Success is not final and failure is not fatal. It's the courage to continue that counts. — Winston Churchill
What lies behind you and what lies before you pale in comparison to what lies within you. — Unknown
The only permission you need is the permission you give yourself. — Unknown
The life worth living requires the risks worth taking. — Unknown
Comparison is the thief of joy, but it's also the enemy of your potential. — Unknown
You don't find time. You create it by eliminating what doesn't matter. — Unknown
The person you're becoming won't accept the excuses the person you were used to make. — Unknown
Small disciplines repeated over time create extraordinary results. — Unknown
Your past is not your prison. It's your preparation. — Unknown
The world rewards action more than intention. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
I told my boss I was feeling burned out.
He said, "Good, that means you're getting somewhere."

My gym trainer asked why I never show up.
I said, "I'm already carrying enough baggage."

I tried meditation to reduce my anxiety.
Now I'm just anxious in silence, which is somehow worse.

My friend asked if I believe in ghosts.
I said, "Only the ones that haunt my browser history."

I went to a motivational seminar about overcoming failure.
The speaker didn't show up.

My therapist asked what my biggest fear is.
I said, "Becoming self-aware enough to change, but not motivated enough to try."

I asked my dad why he never wanted kids.
He said, "I did—I just wish I'd used that information sooner."

My coworker said she's been training for a marathon.
I asked why. She said, "So I can run away faster."

I told my friend he was being paranoid about people judging him.
He said, "See? You're doing it too."

My dentist said I need to floss more.
I said, "I have enough problems without adding dental guilt to the mix."

I got fired for being too honest at work.
Apparently telling your boss his strategic vision is "vibes-based" doesn't go over well.

My mom asked if I'm seeing anyone.
I said, "Yeah, my therapist—twice a week, unfortunately."

I tried to join a support group for procrastinators.
I'll sign up next month.

My friend said he's taking a break from social media.
I said, "Finally, proof that you do take action on your problems."

My doctor said I need to cut back on caffeine.
I said, "I'll think about it after my fourth espresso."

I asked my bank why they kept denying my loan.
They said my credit score was "inspiring but unrealistic."

My partner asked if I ever think about us.
I said, "Yes, and it's usually the problem, not the solution."

I told my parents I'm starting therapy.
My dad said, "Finally, someone's getting paid to listen to you."

My friend asked why I stopped going to the gym.
I said, "I realized self-improvement was just delaying the inevitable."

I applied for a job as a life coach.
They said I didn't have enough experience failing at life.
I'm overqualified, actually.
